description: Commit-aware code context manager for LLMs — MCP server and CLI that builds multi-layered, incremental indexes of code repositories and serves structured context via MCP tools and CLI commands.

userStories.title: Project scaffold, dependencies, and error hierarchy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the full project structure set up with all dependencies declared, package directories created, and the exception hierarchy defined so that all subsequent stories can import from the right places.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml has all runtime dependencies: typer>=0.9, rich>=13.0, mcp>=1.0, anthropic>=0.40, sentence-transformers>=3.0, sqlite-vec>=0.1, aiosqlite>=0.20, pathspec>=0.12, tiktoken>=0.7, tree-sitter>=0.23
pyproject.toml has optional deps for tree-sitter grammars (python, javascript, typescript, go, rust, java, ruby, cpp) and an all-grammars extra
pyproject.toml has dev deps: pytest>=8.0, pytest-asyncio>=0.23, pytest-cov>=5.0, ruff>=0.5, mypy>=1.10
pyproject.toml has [project.scripts] entry: codetex = 'codetex_mcp.cli.app:main'
All package directories exist under src/codetex_mcp/ with __init__.py: cli/, server/, core/, analysis/, llm/, embeddings/, storage/, storage/migrations/, git/, config/
src/codetex_mcp/__main__.py exists and calls cli.app:main

src/codetex_mcp/exceptions.py defines: CodetexError, RepositoryNotFoundError, RepositoryAlreadyExistsError, GitError, GitAuthError (with setup guidance message), IndexError, LLMError, RateLimitError, ConfigError, DatabaseError, EmbeddingError, NoIndexError
All exceptions inherit from CodetexError; GitAuthError inherits from GitError; RateLimitError inherits from LLMError
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc tasks/architecture.md §2, §3.10, §11 for exact structure and dependency versions. Completed: all dirs with __init__.py (including storage/migrations/), full exception hierarchy, pyproject.toml with all deps, __main__.py entry point, typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Configuration settings with TOML loading and env overrides
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Settings dataclass that loads config from TOML file and environment variables so that all modules can access configuration consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: config/settings.py defines a Settings dataclass with fields: data_dir (Path, default ~/.codetex), repos_dir (Path, default data_dir/repos), db_path (Path, default data_dir/codetex.db), llm_provider (str, default 'anthropic'), llm_model (str, default 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'), llm_api_key (str|None), max_file_size_kb (int, default 512), max_concurrent_llm_calls (int, default 5), tier1_rebuild_threshold (float, default 0.10), default_excludes (list[str] with node_modules/, vendor/, __pycache__/, .git/, *.min.js, *.min.css, *.lock, *.map, etc.), embedding_model (str, default 'all-MiniLM-L6-v2')

Settings.load() classmethod loads in order: hardcoded defaults → ~/.codetex/config.toml (if exists) → env vars (CODETEX_DATA_DIR, CODETEX_LLM_PROVIDER, CODETEX_LLM_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CODETEX_MAX_FILE_SIZE_KB, CODETEX_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM, CODETEX_TIER1_THRESHOLD, CODETEX_EMBEDDING_MODEL)
Later sources override earlier ones (env vars win over TOML, TOML wins over defaults)
TOML parsing uses tomllib (stdlib)
Settings.load() creates data_dir and repos_dir if they don't exist
Tests verify default values, TOML override, and env var override
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.9.1 and §8 for config schema. Completed: Settings dataclass with all fields, load() with 3-layer override (defaults → TOML → env vars), TOML parsing via tomllib, directory creation, 27 tests covering defaults/TOML/env/load integration, mypy passes.

userStories.title: Ignore filter for file exclusion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an IgnoreFilter that chains .gitignore, .codetexignore, default excludes, size threshold, and binary detection so that the indexer can skip irrelevant files.
acceptanceCriteria: config/ignore.py defines IgnoreFilter class with __init__(repo_path: Path, default_excludes: list[str], max_file_size_kb: int)
is_excluded(file_path: Path) returns tuple[bool, str|None] where the string is the reason (e.g., 'gitignore', 'codetexignore', 'size threshold', 'binary', 'default exclude')
filter_files(files: list[str]) returns only non-excluded files
Filter chain order: default excludes → .gitignore rules → .codetexignore rules → max file size → binary detection (null byte in first 8KB)
.codetexignore can override .gitignore with !pattern syntax
Uses pathspec library for .gitignore-style pattern matching
Tests cover: default excludes hit, gitignore hit, codetexignore hit, codetexignore override via !, size threshold, binary detection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.9.2 for filter chain details. Completed: IgnoreFilter class in config/ignore.py with 5-stage filter chain (default excludes → .gitignore → .codetexignore → size threshold → binary detection), negation override support via ! patterns in .codetexignore, pathspec library for gitignore-style matching, 22 tests covering all filter stages and chain ordering, mypy passes.

userStories.title: Database foundation with migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Database class with async connection management, WAL mode, sqlite-vec extension loading, and a migration runner, plus the initial migration creating all tables.
acceptanceCriteria: storage/database.py defines Database class with __init__(db_path: Path)
connect() opens aiosqlite connection, enables WAL mode, loads sqlite-vec extension, enables foreign keys
close() closes the connection
execute(sql, params) and executemany(sql, params_list) delegate to the connection
migrate() reads schema_version table (creates it if not exists), finds unapplied SQL files in storage/migrations/ ordered by number prefix, executes them, records new version

storage/migrations/001_initial.sql creates all 6 tables (schema_version, repositories, files, symbols, dependencies, repo_overviews) with all columns, indexes, and foreign keys per architecture doc §4.1
001_initial.sql also creates 2 sqlite-vec virtual tables: vec_file_embeddings and vec_symbol_embeddings with FLOAT[384]
Tests verify: migration runs successfully, tables exist after migration, re-running migrate is idempotent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.7 and §4 for schema details. Completed: Database class in storage/database.py with async connect/close/execute/executemany/migrate methods, WAL mode, sqlite-vec extension loading via sqlite_vec.loadable_path(), foreign keys enabled, migration runner reading numbered SQL files from storage/migrations/ with schema_version tracking. 001_initial.sql creates all 6 tables (schema_version, repositories, files, symbols, dependencies, repo_overviews) with all columns/indexes/foreign keys per architecture spec, plus 2 sqlite-vec virtual tables (vec_file_embeddings, vec_symbol_embeddings) with FLOAT[384]. 16 tests covering connect, WAL mode, foreign keys, sqlite-vec loading, close, execute, executemany, migration creates tables/vector tables/indexes, idempotency, schema version recording, correct columns, foreign key cascades. mypy passes (17 source files).

userStories.title: Repository storage DAO
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CRUD operations for the repositories table so that RepoManager can persist and query repo records.
acceptanceCriteria: storage/repositories.py provides async functions: create_repo(db, name, remote_url, local_path, default_branch) -> Repository, get_repo_by_name(db, name) -> Repository|None, list_repos(db) -> list[Repository], update_indexed_commit(db, repo_id, commit_sha) with last_indexed_at=now, delete_repo(db, repo_id)
Repository is a dataclass with fields matching the repositories table columns
create_repo raises RepositoryAlreadyExistsError on unique constraint violation
Tests verify: create, get by name, list, update commit, delete, duplicate name error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §4.1 repositories table schema. Completed: Repository dataclass with all table columns, async DAO functions (create_repo, get_repo_by_name, list_repos, update_indexed_commit, delete_repo), create_repo raises RepositoryAlreadyExistsError on unique constraint violation, 11 tests covering create/get/list/update/delete/duplicate error, mypy passes (18 source files).

userStories.title: File, symbol, and dependency storage DAOs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CRUD operations for files, symbols, and dependencies tables so that the indexer can persist parsed data and summaries.
acceptanceCriteria: storage/files.py provides: upsert_file(db, repo_id, path, language, lines_of_code, token_count, imports_json) -> file_id, update_file_summary(db, file_id, summary, role), get_file(db, repo_id, path) -> FileRecord|None, list_files(db, repo_id) -> list[FileRecord], delete_file(db, file_id), count_files(db, repo_id) -> int, get_total_tokens(db, repo_id) -> int

storage/symbols.py provides: upsert_symbol(db, file_id, repo_id, name, kind, signature, docstring, start_line, end_line, parameters_json, return_type, calls_json) -> symbol_id, update_symbol_summary(db, symbol_id, summary), get_symbol(db, repo_id, name) -> SymbolRecord|None, list_symbols_by_file(db, file_id) -> list[SymbolRecord], delete_symbols_by_file(db, file_id)
storage/files.py also provides: upsert_dependency(db, repo_id, source_file_id, target_path, imported_names_json), delete_dependencies_by_file(db, file_id)
FileRecord and SymbolRecord are dataclasses matching their table columns
Upsert functions use INSERT OR REPLACE or ON CONFLICT UPDATE
Tests verify: upsert, get, list, update summary, delete, cascade behavior
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §4.1 for files, symbols, dependencies table schemas. Completed: FileRecord and SymbolRecord dataclasses matching table columns, async DAO functions for files (upsert_file, update_file_summary, get_file, list_files, delete_file, count_files, get_total_tokens), symbols (upsert_symbol, update_symbol_summary, get_symbol, list_symbols_by_file, delete_symbols_by_file), and dependencies (upsert_dependency, delete_dependencies_by_file). Files use ON CONFLICT(repo_id, path) for upsert, dependencies use ON CONFLICT(source_file_id, target_path). 28 tests covering upsert/get/list/update/delete/count/cascade behavior. mypy passes (20 source files).

userStories.title: Vector storage DAO
userStories.description: As a developer, I need operations for sqlite-vec virtual tables so that embeddings can be stored and queried for nearest-neighbor search.
acceptanceCriteria: storage/vectors.py provides: upsert_file_embedding(db, file_id, embedding: list[float]), upsert_symbol_embedding(db, symbol_id, embedding: list[float]), delete_file_embedding(db, file_id), delete_symbol_embedding(db, symbol_id), search_file_embeddings(db, query_embedding, limit) -> list[tuple[int, float]], search_symbol_embeddings(db, query_embedding, limit) -> list[tuple[int, float]]
Search functions use sqlite-vec's vec_distance_cosine with embedding MATCH ? and ORDER BY distance
Embeddings are stored as FLOAT[384] vectors
Tests verify: insert embedding, search returns nearest neighbors, delete removes entry
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §4.2 for sqlite-vec virtual table schema and query patterns. Completed: storage/vectors.py with _serialize_f32 helper, async DAO functions (upsert_file_embedding, upsert_symbol_embedding, delete_file_embedding, delete_symbol_embedding, search_file_embeddings, search_symbol_embeddings). Upserts use delete-then-insert pattern because vec0 virtual tables don't support ON CONFLICT. Search uses WHERE embedding MATCH ? ORDER BY distance LIMIT ?. Embeddings serialized via struct.pack as FLOAT[384]. 13 tests covering insert/upsert-replace/delete/search-nearest-neighbors/search-limit/search-empty/delete-nonexistent for both file and symbol embeddings. mypy passes (21 source files).

userStories.title: Git operations wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an async subprocess git wrapper so that core services can clone repos, get HEAD, compute diffs, and list files without depending on gitpython.
acceptanceCriteria: git/operations.py defines GitOperations class with __init__(config: Settings)

clone(url, target_dir) runs git clone via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec, raises GitAuthError on auth failure (detects 'Permission denied' or 'Authentication failed' in stderr), raises GitError on other failures
get_head_commit(repo_path) returns current HEAD SHA via git rev-parse HEAD
get_default_branch(repo_path) returns default branch name
get_remote_url(repo_path) returns origin remote URL or None
diff_commits(repo_path, from_sha, to_sha) returns DiffResult dataclass with added/modified/deleted/renamed lists by parsing git diff --name-status output
list_tracked_files(repo_path) returns list of tracked file paths via git ls-files
is_git_repo(path) returns True if path contains a .git directory
All methods raise GitError with stderr content on unexpected failures
Tests verify: HEAD commit parsing, diff parsing (added/modified/deleted/renamed), is_git_repo check
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.8. Use asyncio.create_subprocess_exec, not gitpython.

userStories.title: Analysis data models and fallback parser
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the analysis data models (FileAnalysis, SymbolInfo, etc.) and a fallback regex-based parser for languages without tree-sitter support.
acceptanceCriteria: analysis/models.py defines: FileAnalysis(path, language, imports, symbols, lines_of_code, token_count), ImportInfo(module, names), SymbolInfo(name, kind, signature, docstring, start_line, end_line, parameters, return_type, calls), ParameterInfo(name, type_annotation, default_value) as dataclasses
SymbolInfo.kind is Literal['function', 'method', 'class', 'variable', 'constant']
analysis/fallback_parser.py defines FallbackParser with parse(content: str, language: str|None) -> FileAnalysis
FallbackParser extracts: lines matching def/function/class/func/fn patterns as symbols, import/require/include lines as imports, first contiguous comment block as file docstring, line count
Token count is computed via tiktoken (cl100k_base encoding)
Tests verify: Python function extraction, JavaScript function extraction, import extraction, line count, token count
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.4.3 and §3.4.4.

userStories.title: Tree-sitter parser and unified dispatcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a tree-sitter AST parser with on-demand grammar loading and a unified Parser dispatcher that falls back to regex when tree-sitter is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: analysis/tree_sitter.py defines TreeSitterParser with is_language_supported(language) -> bool and parse(content, language) -> FileAnalysis
Grammar loading is on-demand: attempts to import tree_sitter_python, tree_sitter_javascript etc. on first call per language; caches result
If grammar import fails (ImportError), reports language as unsupported
Extracts: function/method signatures with parameters and return types, class definitions with bases, import statements, docstrings
analysis/parser.py defines Parser with __init__(tree_sitter_parser, fallback_parser), parse_file(path, content, language) -> FileAnalysis, detect_language(path) -> str|None
detect_language maps file extensions to language names (.py->python, .js->javascript, .ts->typescript, .go->go, .rs->rust, .java->java, .rb->ruby, .cpp/.cc/.h->cpp, etc.)
parse_file: detects language, tries tree-sitter, falls back to FallbackParser if unsupported
Tests verify: language detection from extensions, tree-sitter parse of Python file (if grammar installed), fallback when grammar unavailable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.4.1 and §3.4.2. Grammars are optional pip extras.

userStories.title: LLM rate limiter, prompts, and provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LLM integration layer: rate limiter for concurrency control, prompt templates for all three tiers, and the provider abstraction with Anthropic implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: llm/rate_limiter.py defines RateLimiter with __init__(max_concurrent: int, base_delay: float, max_delay: float), acquire(), release(), handle_rate_limit() methods using asyncio.Semaphore and exponential backoff with jitter
llm/prompts.py defines tier1_prompt(repo_name, directory_tree, file_summaries, technologies) -> str, tier2_prompt(file_path, content, symbols) -> str, tier3_prompt(symbol, file_context) -> str
Each prompt template instructs the LLM to respond in structured markdown format
Tier 2 prompt asks for: purpose summary, public interfaces, dependencies, role classification
Tier 3 prompt asks for: description, parameter details, return type, call relationships
llm/provider.py defines abstract LLMProvider with summarize(prompt, system) -> str and summarize_batch(prompts, system) -> list[str]
AnthropicProvider implements LLMProvider using the anthropic SDK with configurable api_key and model
summarize_batch uses RateLimiter for concurrency control and retries on RateLimitError with exponential backoff
Tests verify: rate limiter concurrency limiting, prompt templates produce non-empty strings, AnthropicProvider initialization
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.5. Default model: claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929. Default concurrency: 5.

userStories.title: Embeddings module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Embedder class that wraps sentence-transformers for local embedding generation with lazy model loading.
acceptanceCriteria: embeddings/embedder.py defines Embedder class with MODEL_NAME = 'all-MiniLM-L6-v2' and DIMENSIONS = 384
Model is lazily loaded on first call to embed() or embed_batch() — not at construction time
embed(text: str) -> list[float] returns a 384-dimension vector
embed_batch(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]] returns vectors for all inputs
Returned vectors are normalized (suitable for cosine similarity)
If model download fails, raises EmbeddingError with helpful message
Tests verify: embed returns 384-dim vector, embed_batch returns correct count, lazy loading (model not loaded until first call)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.6. CPU-only, ~23MB download on first use.

userStories.title: RepoManager core service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RepoManager to handle repository lifecycle (clone, register local, list, get, remove) so that CLI and MCP can manage repos.
acceptanceCriteria: core/repo_manager.py defines RepoManager with __init__(db, git, config)
add_remote(url) clones repo to config.repos_dir/<name>/, records in DB, returns Repository. Name derived from URL basename minus .git suffix
add_local(path) validates path is a git repo, records in DB without cloning, returns Repository
Both add methods raise RepositoryAlreadyExistsError if repo name already registered
list_repos() returns all registered repositories with their status
get_repo(name) returns Repository or raises RepositoryNotFoundError
remove_repo(name) deletes from DB (does not delete cloned files)
URL detection: contains '://' or starts with 'git@' → add_remote; else → add_local
Tests verify: add remote (mocked git clone), add local, duplicate detection, list, get, remove, URL vs path detection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.3.1.

userStories.title: ContextStore and SearchEngine core services
userStories.description: As a developer, I need ContextStore to read indexed context and SearchEngine to perform semantic similarity search so that CLI and MCP can query the index.
acceptanceCriteria: core/context_store.py defines ContextStore with __init__(db)
get_repo_overview(repo_id) returns Tier 1 overview string from repo_overviews table, or None
get_file_context(repo_id, file_path) returns FileContext dataclass (summary, role, imports, symbols, LOC, token_count) or None
get_symbol_detail(repo_id, symbol_name) returns SymbolDetail dataclass (signature, summary, parameters, return_type, calls, file_path, start_line) or None
get_repo_status(repo_id) returns RepoStatus dataclass (indexed_commit, current_head via git, is_stale, files_indexed, symbols_indexed, total_tokens, last_indexed_at)
core/search_engine.py defines SearchEngine with __init__(db, embedder)
search(repo_id, query, max_results=10) embeds query, searches vec_file_embeddings and vec_symbol_embeddings, merges and ranks by cosine similarity, returns list[SearchResult]
SearchResult has: kind (file|symbol), path, name, summary, score
Tests verify: get_repo_overview returns stored overview, search returns ranked results, empty index returns empty list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.3.4 and §3.3.5.

userStories.title: Indexer — full index pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Indexer to orchestrate the 9-step full indexing pipeline so that repositories can be analyzed, summarized, and embedded.
acceptanceCriteria: core/indexer.py defines Indexer with __init__(db, git, parser, llm, embedder, config)
index(repo, path_filter=None, dry_run=False, on_progress=None) -> IndexResult
Step 1: discovers files via git ls-files + IgnoreFilter + path_filter
Step 2: parses each file via Parser (tree-sitter or fallback)
Step 3: upserts file/symbol/dependency records, commits per batch
Steps 4-5: LLM summarizes Tier 2 (file summaries) via rate-limited batch, stores results
Steps 6-7: LLM summarizes Tier 3 (symbol summaries) for functions and classes, stores results
Step 8: generates embeddings for file and symbol summaries, stores in sqlite-vec
Step 9: generates Tier 1 overview via single LLM call, stores in repo_overviews, updates indexed_commit
dry_run=True runs Steps 1-2 only and returns estimated LLM calls and token counts without API calls
on_progress callback called with (current, total, file_path) for progress reporting
Interrupt-resumable: per-batch commits, re-run skips files already indexed for current commit
Returns IndexResult(files_indexed, symbols_extracted, llm_calls_made, tokens_used, duration_seconds, commit_sha)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.3.2 and §5.1 for the full pipeline specification.

userStories.title: Syncer — incremental sync pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Syncer to orchestrate the 7-step incremental sync pipeline so that index updates are efficient when commits change.
acceptanceCriteria: core/syncer.py defines Syncer with __init__(db, git, parser, llm, embedder, config)
sync(repo, path_filter=None, dry_run=False) -> SyncResult
Step 1: compares indexed_commit with current HEAD; returns already_current=True if equal
Step 2: computes git diff, applies IgnoreFilter and path_filter
Step 3: deletes file/symbol/dependency/vector records for deleted files
Step 4: re-analyzes added + modified files (parse, store structure, LLM Tier 2+3 summaries)
Step 5: updates embeddings for changed files/symbols, removes vectors for deleted
Step 6: rebuilds Tier 1 if changed_ratio >= tier1_rebuild_threshold
Step 7: updates indexed_commit and last_indexed_at
dry_run=True computes diff and estimates without making LLM calls
Returns SyncResult(already_current, files_added, files_modified, files_deleted, llm_calls_made, tokens_used, tier1_rebuilt, old_commit, new_commit, duration_seconds)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.3.3 and §5.2 for the sync pipeline specification.

userStories.title: Application wiring — AppContext and create_app
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the AppContext dataclass and create_app factory function to wire all components together so that CLI and MCP server can bootstrap the app.
acceptanceCriteria: core/__init__.py defines AppContext dataclass with fields: settings, db, git, parser, llm, embedder, repo_manager, indexer, syncer, context_store, search_engine

create_app(settings=None) async factory: loads Settings if not provided, creates Database and runs migrations, creates GitOperations, Parser (tree-sitter + fallback), AnthropicProvider, Embedder, RepoManager, ContextStore, SearchEngine, Indexer, Syncer
Returns fully wired AppContext
LLM and embedder are constructed but defer heavy init to first use (lazy loading)
Tests verify: create_app returns AppContext with all fields populated, database is migrated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §10 for the full wiring specification.

userStories.title: CLI commands — add, list, status, config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the basic CLI commands (add, list, status, config) implemented with Typer so I can manage repos and configuration from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/app.py defines a Typer app with name='codetex'
'codetex add <target>' clones remote URLs or registers local paths, prints success with repo name
'codetex list' displays table of registered repos with name, remote_url, indexed_commit, last_indexed_at using rich
'codetex status <repo>' shows: indexed commit, current HEAD, is_stale, files indexed, symbols indexed, total tokens, last sync time
'codetex config show' displays current Settings as formatted table
'codetex config set <key> <value>' writes to ~/.codetex/config.toml
All CodetexError subclasses caught at top level and displayed as 'Error: <message>' with non-zero exit code
main() function creates app context and runs the Typer app
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.1 and §7 for CLI command mapping.

userStories.title: CLI commands — index, sync, context
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the index, sync, and context CLI commands so I can build indexes, sync changes, and query context from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: 'codetex index <repo> [--path P] [--dry-run]' triggers Indexer.index with rich progress bar showing file count/percentage/current file
index --dry-run shows: files to index, estimated LLM calls, estimated token count, without making API calls
'codetex sync <repo> [--path P] [--dry-run]' triggers Syncer.sync, displays summary of changes
sync --dry-run shows what would change and estimated cost
'codetex context <repo>' returns Tier 1 overview formatted as terminal markdown via rich
'codetex context <repo> --file <path>' returns Tier 2 file summary
'codetex context <repo> --symbol <name>' returns Tier 3 symbol detail
'codetex context <repo> --query <question>' runs semantic search and displays ranked results
'codetex serve' starts the MCP server (delegates to server/mcp_server.py)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.1 and §7 for CLI command mapping.

userStories.title: MCP server with 7 tools
userStories.description: As a developer using an MCP client, I need a FastMCP server exposing 7 tools so that LLMs can query codebase context on demand via stdio transport.
acceptanceCriteria: server/mcp_server.py creates FastMCP('codetex') server
get_repo_overview(repo_name) tool returns Tier 1 markdown overview
get_file_context(repo_name, file_path) tool returns Tier 2 file summary
get_symbol_detail(repo_name, symbol_name) tool returns Tier 3 symbol detail
search_context(repo_name, query, max_results=10) tool returns ranked search results
get_repo_status(repo_name) tool returns index status (indexed commit, staleness, file count)
sync_repo(repo_name) tool triggers incremental sync and returns change summary
list_repos() tool returns all registered repos with status
All tools return structured markdown strings suitable for LLM consumption
Errors return MCP error responses with isError=True and descriptive messages
Server uses stdio transport and can be configured in Claude Code MCP settings
Server calls create_app() once at startup and reuses the AppContext
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference architecture doc §3.2 and §6 for tool signatures and response shapes.
